description:
## Problem

The current accessibility dimension in multi-dimensional health scoring relies entirely on CEM metadata, which captures ~20% of actual accessibility implementation. Cross-library validation against 9 major WC libraries (Shoelace, FAST, Ionic, Vaadin, Carbon, Material Web, PatternFly, Fluent, Lion) shows 0% pass rate on 4 of 8 checks (hasAriaRole, hasAriaAttributes, hasFormAssociation, hasKeyboardEvents) because the CEM standard doesn't require this data.

Meanwhile, real components have rich accessibility ŌĆö ARIA template bindings, keyboard handlers, focus management, form internals, live regions ŌĆö all in source code that helixir can read.

## Solution

Build a source-code accessibility scanner that reads component files via CEM module `path` field and detects real patterns:

### Detection Categories

1. **Template ARIA Bindings** ŌĆö Parse render()/template for `aria-*` attributes, `role=` assignments, `aria-live`, `aria-modal`
2. **Keyboard Support** ŌĆö `@keydown`/`addEventListener('keydown')`, key switch statements (Space, Enter, Escape, Arrow)
3. **Focus Management** ŌĆö `focus()` calls, focus trapping patterns, `aria-activedescendant`, roving tabindex
4. **Form Internals** ŌĆö `attachInternals()`, `static formAssociated`, validation callbacks
5. **Screen Reader Support** ŌĆö `aria-hidden`, `.sr-only`/`.visually-hidden`, label/description ID chains
6. **Live Regions** ŌĆö `aria-live`, `role="alert"`, `role="status"`, `aria-atomic`
7. **Semantic HTML** ŌĆö Native element usage vs custom elements needing explicit ARIA

### Architecture

- New analyzer: `packages/core/src/handlers/analyzers/source-accessibility.ts`
- Uses TypeScript compiler API (already available) or regex-based scanning for template patterns
- Maps CEM module `path` ŌåÆ source file ŌåÆ parse for patterns
- Returns standard `AnalyzerResult` with sub-metrics per category
- Replaces CEM-only heuristics as the primary accessibility dimension
- Must work with ANY web component framework: Lit, FAST, Stencil, vanilla, Haunted, etc.

### Framework Detection Patterns

- **Lit**: `html\`...\`` tagged templates, `@keydown=`, `.aria-label=`
- **FAST**: `@attr`, template functions, `$observe`
- **Stencil**: JSX-style `aria-label={...}`, `@Listen('keydown')`
- **Vanilla**: `setAttribute('aria-*')`, `addEventListener('keydown')`

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Scanner reads source files referenced by CEM module paths
- [ ] Detects all 7 accessibility categories above
- [ ] Works with Lit, FAST, Stencil, and vanilla WC patterns
- [ ] Integrates with multi-dimensional health scoring as the Accessibility dimension
- [ ] Helix library scores Ōēź70% on accessibility (reflecting real implementation)
- [ ] Cross-library validation shows meaningful differentiation (not all F's)
- [ ] Full test coverage for the new analyzer
- [ ] Falls back gracefully when source files aren't accessible
title: feat: source-level accessibility scanner for universal web component a11y scoring
